intro: Artemisia saissanica, also known as Mongolian wormwood, is a perennial plant species belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae), native to the mountainous regions of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Artemisia saissanica include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditional herbal preparations made with Artemisia saissanica include tea, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to alleviate symptoms of various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
